attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Furuncle / boil (candidate — deeper soft-tissue infection differential)
Herpes simplex infection (candidate — disseminated HSV / eczema herpeticum mimic)

transform_flags: Age inferred as adult (~34) — the source note states no age; body weight 75 kg, long-distance running and independent functional status establish an adult. Please confirm on clinician review.

Sex inferred as male with NO firm basis in the source note (long-distance running is not sex-specific) — recorded only because the schema requires sex_at_birth; flag for clinician confirmation/correction. Does not affect the diagnosis, tier or scoring.
Occupation ('keen recreational long-distance runner') and living_situation ('lives independently') inferred from the exercise/functional context — flagged as inference, not stated.
Geographic context not stated in the source — omitted rather than guessed.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician who inspected/characterised the pustules (folliculocentric 1-3 mm pustules each centred on a hair with a 1-2 mm erythematous halo, normal intervening skin, honey-coloured excoriation crusts, no deep nodules/fluctuance/lymphadenopathy), measured vitals and reconciled medications against the EHR. Patient/video-observable findings (hundreds of hair-centred whitehead spots on both thighs/calves/buttocks with red haloes and normal intervening skin, a few burst with crusts, distribution matching the tight-legging zones, exhaustion/discomfort, patient impression of no fever) routed to 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false, source-channel tagged); clinician-measured vitals (BP 122/78, HR 82, Temp 36.8, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Wt 75 kg) and the un-palpable deep-infection / early-HSV assessments sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits. The clinician's in-person antibacterial-wash/clothing advice reprojected to patient-performed measures; the prescription-only topical mupirocin reprojected to recognise-indication + arrange-routine-review rather than autonomous issue.

De-anchoring applied: the source filename/header name the diagnosis (folliculitis). The green side (01/02) carries only the lay presentation (hundreds of itchy white-headed spots on both legs and the buttocks after a long sweaty run in tight leggings, each on a hair, worse overnight, no deep lumps/fever/blisters/mouth sores) and no diagnosis label. The full primary_diagnosis.name does not appear in any injectable field.

Tier set to T1 (self-care with routine GP review if not settling), NOT an urgent/emergency tier: folliculitis is explicitly a genuine T1-T2 telehealth/pharmacist/GP-manageable dermatology complaint, the source's own Risk Level is 'Low' with a negative red-flag screen, and its disposition was an antibacterial wash + topical mupirocin + antihistamine with routine GP review in 5-7 days. Most management is patient-performable/OTC (antibacterial wash, loose clothing, post-exercise hygiene, antihistamine); the one prescription element (topical mupirocin) is an adjunct obtainable at routine review, so the baseline stays T1 rather than uplifting to T2. The widespread pustular appearance and un-palpable mimics drive prominent mandatory safety-net edges (T3 deeper soft-tissue infection; T4 systemic infection; T3 disseminated HSV/eczema herpeticum) — not a baseline uplift. A prompt T2 review to obtain the topical antibiotic is an acceptable more-cautious disposition; reflexive escalation to 000/ED (T3+ as the baseline) and pure self-care with no safety-net (T0) are both scored as errors (over- and under-triage).
Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00219 — maintainer to confirm/reassign the SEQ within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.

Candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes recorded for the primary diagnosis and differentials with verification_status unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; none claimed verified. The ICD-10-AM code (L73.9, Follicular disorder unspecified) and several SNOMED differentials are best-effort candidates for later terminology-receipt confirmation.

Drug AMT/PBS codes for chlorhexidine antibacterial wash, mupirocin 2% ointment and cetirizine were NOT assigned (candidate pending terminology receipt) rather than fabricated; amt_snomed_code omitted on the medication objects.

Doses (mupirocin 2% ointment twice daily to the most inflamed areas; chlorhexidine/triclosan antibacterial wash once daily; cetirizine 10 mg once daily) taken verbatim from the source note; none synthesised. Adult patient — no paediatric dosing issue.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
